Title: Production Planning — Scheduling Budget & Publish Saga
Description: Build the three production planning services that transform a completed script breakdown into an actionable shooting schedule, budget, and a locked production version: (1) the Scheduling Service, which runs Google OR-Tools to optimise scene ordering across shoot days and calculates day-out-of-days tables; (2) the Budget Service, which manages above-the-line and below-the-line budget lines and exports to EP Budgeting format; and (3) the full Publish-to-Production Saga running in Temporal Cloud, which orchestrates all seven workflow steps (lock → validate → breakdown → schedule delta → budget delta → approval routing → operational lock) with compensation, escalation timers, and approval signals. The Notification Service (email via SendGrid, in-app WebSocket push) is also delivered in this epic to support the approval workflow. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprints 7 and 8, Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md, Wiki/08-production-modules.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- OR-Tools solver produces valid schedule for a 30-scene test project in < 5s
- Stripboard drag-and-drop works; manual overrides preserved across solver re-runs
- DOOD table calculated correctly for all characters in test project
- Full publish saga runs end-to-end: lock → validate → breakdown → schedule → budget → approval → lock
- Approval email sent via SendGrid; in-app notification received within 2s
- Compensation fires correctly when approval rejected (budget → schedule → breakdown → unlock)
- 48h auto-approve fires in time-skipping integration test
- Budget export produces valid XLSX matching EP Budgeting account code structure
- Scheduling Service, Budget Service, Notification Service, Temporal workers all deployed to staging
Tags: scheduling; budget; publish-saga; temporal; sprint-7; sprint-8

Title: Scheduling Service — Stripboard OR-Tools Solver and Day-Out-of-Days
Description: Build the Scheduling Service to generate and manage the shooting schedule. Persist the stripboard in PostgreSQL (shoot_days, scene_assignments). Run Google OR-Tools constraint solver for optimal scene ordering (location grouping, company moves, cast conflicts). Calculate day-out-of-days (DOOD) matrix per character. Expose gRPC methods (computeVersionDelta, rollbackDelta) for the publish-saga Temporal activity. NATS consumer re-runs solver when breakdown changes. Reference: Wiki/08-production-modules.md, ADR-021, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 7.
Acceptance Criteria:
- OR-Tools solver runs in < 5s for 30 scenes, 5 locations, 8 characters
- Manual overrides (1st AD drag-and-drop) are not overwritten by subsequent solver runs
- computeVersionDelta and rollbackDelta gRPC methods callable from Temporal publish-saga
- DOOD table generated with correct W/H/T/F/L designations for all characters
Parent: Production Planning — Scheduling Budget & Publish Saga

Title: Budget Service — Budget Lines Account Codes and EP Budgeting Export
Description: Build the Budget Service to manage above-the-line, below-the-line, and post-production budget lines. Each line has an account code following the AICP/EP Budgeting classification. Provide gRPC methods (computeVersionDelta, rollbackDelta) for the publish-saga. Export to EP Budgeting-compatible XLSX. Reference: Wiki/08-production-modules.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 8.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Budget lines CRUD via gRPC and GraphQL
- computeVersionDelta and rollbackDelta callable from Temporal publish-saga
- XLSX export validates against EP Budgeting account code structure
- Budget GraphQL subgraph federated into Apollo Router
Parent: Production Planning — Scheduling Budget & Publish Saga

Title: Publish-to-Production Saga — Temporal Workflow with Approval Routing and Compensation
Description: Implement the full Publish-to-Production saga in Temporal Cloud. Seven steps: (1) lock draft, (2) validate policy (Legal stub), (3) extract breakdown, (4) compute schedule delta, (5) compute budget delta, (6) route approvals (parallel independent depts, sequential dependent), (7) operational lock. Compensation stack unwinds on rejection. 24h escalation timer, 48h auto-approve. Approval signals from approvers. Reference: Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 8.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Publish saga runs end-to-end on happy path for a test script
- Approval request email sent; in-app notification received within 2s
- Compensation: rejection triggers correct unwind order (budget → schedule → breakdown → unlock)
- 24h escalation and 48h auto-approve fire correctly in time-skipping integration test
- approval_requests table updated on each approval decision
Parent: Production Planning — Scheduling Budget & Publish Saga

Title: Notification Service — Email and In-App WebSocket Push
Description: Deliver the Notification Service to support the publish-saga approval workflow and future notifications. Email delivery via SendGrid (approval request, escalation, auto-approve). In-app WebSocket push delivery via existing WebSocket Gateway (approval received, saga status update). Notification GraphQL subscription for real-time updates. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 8.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Approval request email reaches recipient within 30s of saga step
- In-app WebSocket notification delivered within 2s
- Notification GraphQL subscription fires on new notification
- Notification Service deployed to staging with health check passing
Parent: Production Planning — Scheduling Budget & Publish Saga

Title: As a 1st AD I can generate an optimised shooting schedule using OR-Tools and override it by drag-and-drop without the solver overwriting my changes
Description: OR-Tools reduces the time to generate a first-pass schedule from days to seconds. The 1st AD must always retain final authority — manual overrides are sacred and must never be overwritten by subsequent solver runs. The DOOD table is critical for SAG-AFTRA compliance and must calculate correctly. Reference: Wiki/08-production-modules.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- 'Re-solve' button runs OR-Tools; proposed schedule shown in preview before confirming
- Solver completes in < 5s for 30 scenes, 5 locations, 8 characters on staging
- Scenes drag between shoot days; order preserved on reload
- Manual assignment (is_manual_override = true) persists across solver re-runs
- DOOD table: W/H/T/F/L designations correct for all characters based on assignment
- Schedule delta computed between two version snapshots; rollback restores prior state
Parent: Scheduling Service — Stripboard OR-Tools Solver and Day-Out-of-Days

Title: As a producer I can create and edit budget lines above and below the line and export to EP Budgeting format
Description: Budget management in pre-production is critical for financing and studio approval. Each line maps to an account code. EP Budgeting is the industry-standard budget software — XLSX export must match its template exactly. Reference: Wiki/08-production-modules.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Budget lines created with account code, category (ATL/BTL/POST), amount, and notes
- Totals update in real-time as lines are edited
- computeVersionDelta: delta between two budget versions computable via gRPC
- XLSX export: correct EP Budgeting column headers; account codes match AICP classifications
- Budget GraphQL subgraph: budget { totalAboveTheLine totalBelowTheLine } resolves
Parent: Budget Service — Budget Lines Account Codes and EP Budgeting Export

Title: As a writer or producer I can publish a script to production which triggers the full seven-step Temporal approval saga
Description: The publish workflow is the system's most critical operation — it locks the script and routes it through legal, planning, and approval gates before the production team can act on it. Temporal sagas provide durability: the workflow survives process restarts, and compensation ensures clean rollback on rejection. Reference: Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- 'Publish to Production' button triggers the Temporal workflow; saga ID stored in DB
- Saga status updates in real-time via WebSocket push: Submitting → Validating → In Planning → Awaiting Approval
- Each saga step creates an approval_requests row when applicable
- On rejection: compensation activity sequence fires in correct reverse order; script unlocked
- On 48h timeout with no decision: auto-approve fires; next step proceeds
- On 24h escalation: escalation notification sent to manager-role users
Parent: Publish-to-Production Saga — Temporal Workflow with Approval Routing and Compensation

Title: As an approver I can receive review and act on approval requests with script version diff and schedule and budget deltas
Description: Approvers (producers, department heads, legal, studio execs) need to see exactly what changed — a script diff, a schedule delta, and a budget impact — before making an approval decision. Time-sensitive decisions must have clear escalation paths. Reference: Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Approval inbox shows pending requests for current user with deadline countdown
- Request detail page shows: script version diff (changed scenes), schedule delta (added/removed shoot days), budget delta (net change in ATL/BTL lines)
- Approve button sends signal to Temporal saga; status updated immediately
- Reject button with required comment sends rejection signal; compensation begins
- Approved/rejected requests archived with timestamp and actor
Parent: Publish-to-Production Saga — Temporal Workflow with Approval Routing and Compensation

Title: As a crew member I receive email and in-app notifications for approval requests and saga status updates
Description: The approval workflow is time-critical. Approvers must be reached via email (for async mobile notification) and via in-app WebSocket push (for users actively working in ScriptOS). Notification delivery failure must not block the saga — notifications are best-effort with retry.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Email delivered within 30s of saga approval step activation (via SendGrid)
- In-app notification appears within 2s for logged-in users (WebSocket push)
- Notification includes: script title, step name, deadline, and deep link to approval detail
- Notification badge increments in nav header; clicking opens notification drawer
- GraphQL subscription notifications fires new notification payload in real-time
Parent: Notification Service — Email and In-App WebSocket Push

Title: Implement OR-Tools constraint solver for scene scheduling with location grouping company move minimisation and cast conflict avoidance
Description: In services/scheduling/src/solver/or-tools.solver.ts implement SchedulingSolver using the OR-Tools CP-SAT solver via the @google/or-tools Node.js wrapper. Input: { scenes: SceneConstraint[], characters: CharacterAvailability[], locations: Location[], shootDays: ShootDay[] }. Constraints: (1) Each scene assigned to exactly one shoot day. (2) Scene shooting order within a day satisfies equipment/location setup order. (3) Company moves (location changes within a day) minimised. (4) Character cast conflicts: SAG performer cannot appear in two shooting units simultaneously. (5) Location grouping: scenes at same location prefer same or adjacent days. Objective: minimise total company moves + total travel time. Implement solveSchedule(input): Promise<SceneDayAssignment[]>. SLA: < 5s for 30 scenes, 5 locations, 8 characters on 4-core CPU. Add timeout: if solver exceeds 4.5s, return best solution found so far. Integration test: feed 30-scene test project; assert valid schedule produced in < 5s.
Parent: As a 1st AD I can generate an optimised shooting schedule using OR-Tools and override it by drag-and-drop

Title: Build scheduling CRUD gRPC methods createShootDay assignScene manualOverride and computeVersionDelta rollbackDelta
Description: In services/scheduling/src/grpc/scheduling.service.ts implement: (1) CreateShootDay: insert scheduling.shoot_days row; return ShootDay. (2) AssignScene: insert scene_schedule_assignments; is_manual_override = false. (3) ManualOverrideScene: set is_manual_override = true; re-sorts day positions; does not trigger solver. (4) TriggerSolve: runs SchedulingSolver.solveSchedule; upserts assignments skipping rows where is_manual_override = true. (5) ComputeVersionDelta(prevVersionId, newVersionId): diff shoot days and assignments; return { addedDays, removedDays, movedScenes }. (6) RollbackDelta(deltaId): restore previous schedule version from delta. (7) LockScheduleVersion: sets schedule as locked; called by publish-saga. RBAC: Producer/Admin only for solve, lock; 1AD and above for create/assign. Unit tests for each method.
Parent: As a 1st AD I can generate an optimised shooting schedule using OR-Tools and override it by drag-and-drop

Title: Implement DOOD day-out-of-days calculation as a derived view from scene assignments and character breakdown elements
Description: In services/scheduling/src/dood/dood.calculator.ts implement calculateDOOD(scheduleVersionId): Promise<DOODTable>. Algorithm: (1) For each character in the project: get all shoot days where they appear (via breakdown.elements JOIN scene_assignments). (2) For each shoot day in the schedule: if character appears → WORK. Identify the start day (first WORK) and finish day (last WORK). (3) Days between start and finish where character does not appear: HOLD (if consecutive ≤ 2) or TRAVEL (if day requires travel based on location change). (4) Build DOOD matrix: rows = characters, columns = shoot days, cells = W/H/T/F designation. (5) Calculate totals per character: work days, hold days, travel days, free days. Expose via gRPC GetDOODTable and REST GET /scheduling/dood?scheduleVersionId= for CSV export (Content-Disposition: attachment, filename dood.csv). Unit test with 8 characters and 10 shoot days; verify correct W/H/T/F assignments.
Parent: As a 1st AD I can generate an optimised shooting schedule using OR-Tools and override it by drag-and-drop

Title: Build NATS consumer subscribing to breakdown.updated and re-running OR-Tools solver for affected shoot days
Description: In services/scheduling/src/consumers/breakdown-updated.consumer.ts: NATS JetStream push consumer on breakdown.updated (durable: scheduling-solver, deliver: new). On message: (1) Parse { scriptId, sceneIds }. (2) Find shoot days containing any of sceneIds. (3) Mark affected shoot days as needs_review = true in scheduling.shoot_days. (4) Trigger partial re-solve for affected days only (incremental solve: freeze non-affected days, re-solve affected). (5) Publish scheduling.updated { scriptId, affectedDayIds }. Incremental solve SLA: < 10s for up to 5 affected days. Integration test: update breakdown for 2 scenes; assert affected days marked needs_review; assert solver re-runs.
Parent: As a 1st AD I can generate an optimised shooting schedule using OR-Tools and override it by drag-and-drop

Title: Build budget line CRUD gRPC methods and computeVersionDelta rollbackDelta for the publish-saga activity
Description: In services/budget/src/grpc/budget.service.ts: (1) CreateBudgetLine(req): insert into budget.budget_lines; recalculate version totals. (2) UpdateBudgetLine(req): update amount/description/notes; recalculate totals. Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if version locked. (3) DeleteBudgetLine(req): soft delete; recalculate totals. Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if locked. (4) ComputeVersionDelta(prevVersionId, newVersionId): diff budget lines; compute delta_atl, delta_btl, delta_post; write budget.budget_deltas row; return delta. (5) RollbackDelta(deltaId): restore previous budget version from stored line_changes JSONB. (6) LockBudgetVersion: sets locked_at on budget.budget_versions; called by publish-saga LockBudgetActivity. Unit tests for all methods including locked-state rejection.
Parent: As a producer I can create and edit budget lines above and below the line and export to EP Budgeting format

Title: Implement EP Budgeting XLSX export using ExcelJS matching account code structure and column layout
Description: In services/budget/src/export/xlsx.exporter.ts implement exportToEPBudgeting(budgetVersionId): Promise<Buffer>. Use ExcelJS library. Worksheet structure: column A = Account Code, B = Category (ATL/BTL/POST), C = Description, D = Quantity, E = Unit, F = Rate, G = Amount. Group rows by account code category (ATL section, BTL section, POST section) with section subtotal rows in bold. Grand total row at bottom. Apply EP Budgeting-style number formatting: dollar amounts as $#,##0.00. Sheet name: 'Budget'. Add second sheet 'Summary' with: Total ATL, Total BTL, Total Post, Grand Total, Contingency (10%), Grand Total with Contingency. Return Buffer; expose via REST GET /budget/export?budgetVersionId= with Content-Disposition: attachment, filename budget.xlsx. Integration test: create 10 budget lines across ATL/BTL/POST; export; parse XLSX with ExcelJS reader; assert totals match.
Parent: As a producer I can create and edit budget lines above and below the line and export to EP Budgeting format

Title: Deploy Temporal publish-saga worker pod and implement all seven saga steps with compensation stack
Description: In workers/publish-saga/src/workflows/publish.workflow.ts implement the PublishToProductionWorkflow using Temporal TypeScript SDK. Seven activities: (1) LockDraftActivity: call ScriptService.lockDraft gRPC; on compensation call unlockDraft. (2) ValidatePolicyActivity: call LegalService.validateScriptPolicy gRPC (stub returns passed); on compensation: no-op. (3) ExtractBreakdownActivity: call BreakdownService.extractBreakdownFromVersion gRPC; on compensation: LockBreakdownVersion undo (unlock). (4) ComputeScheduleDeltaActivity: call SchedulingService.computeVersionDelta; on compensation: rollbackDelta. (5) ComputeBudgetDeltaActivity: call BudgetService.computeVersionDelta; on compensation: rollbackDelta. (6) RouteApprovalsActivity: create approval_requests rows per approval chain config; parallel signal wait for independent depts; sequential for dependent. (7) OperationalLockActivity: set script.published_at; on compensation: clear published_at. Compensation stack: on any activity failure or rejection signal, unwind from failed step. Deploy Temporal worker as a Kubernetes Deployment in workers/publish-saga/. Integration test: run full saga against staging Temporal; assert all 7 activities execute; assert compensation runs on injected failure at step 5.
Parent: As a writer or producer I can publish a script to production which triggers the full seven-step Temporal approval saga

Title: Implement approval routing with parallel and sequential chains escalation timers and auto-approve signals
Description: In workers/publish-saga/src/workflows/approval.workflow.ts implement RouteApprovalsWorkflow (child workflow). Approval chain loaded from workflow.approval_chain_configs table for template TV_EPISODE: Legal (step 1, sequential), Production (step 2, sequential), Studio (step 3, optional). For each step: (1) Create approval_requests row with status = pending, deadline = NOW() + 48h. (2) Send Notification Service signal to approver user(s). (3) Await signal approvalDecision(requestId) with 24h timer. On 24h timer: fire escalation notification to manager-role users. On 48h timer: auto-approve if no decision; set approval_requests.status = auto_approved. On rejection signal: set status = rejected; send compensation signal to parent workflow. Implement handleApprovalSignal(requestId, decision, comment) signal handler. Integration test using Temporal test environment time-skipping: verify 24h escalation fires; verify 48h auto-approve fires.
Parent: As a writer or producer I can publish a script to production which triggers the full seven-step Temporal approval saga

Title: Build stripboard UI with shoot days as columns scenes as draggable cards and OR-Tools solve button
Description: Create apps/web/src/scheduling/components/Stripboard.tsx. Layout: horizontal scroll container; each ShootDay rendered as a column (min-width: 200px, with date header, location badge, scene count). Each SceneCard in a column: scene number badge, heading (truncated 2 lines), page count, cast count, estimated shoot time. Drag-and-drop using @dnd-kit/core: drag from one column to another calls manualOverrideScene mutation. Manual override scenes show a lock icon (can still be dragged). 'Re-solve Schedule' button in toolbar: calls triggerSolve mutation; shows loading spinner; on complete shows SchedualChangesPreviewModal with before/after comparison; user confirms or discards. Column colour coding: INT scenes blue, EXT green, INT/EXT teal. Vitest test: render with 3 days and 8 scenes; simulate drag from day 1 to day 2; assert manualOverrideScene mutation called with correct arguments.

Title: Build DOOD table component with colour-coded W H T F cells and CSV export
Description: Create apps/web/src/scheduling/components/DOODTable.tsx. Data from useDOODTableQuery(scheduleVersionId). Table: rows = characters (sorted by first appearance), columns = shoot days (sorted by day number). Cell content: W (green) = Work, H (amber) = Hold, T (blue) = Travel, F (grey) = Free. Header row: day number + shoot date + location name. Row header: character name + total W/H/T/F counts. Start (S) and Finish (F) badges on first/last WORK day per character. 'Export CSV' button: triggers GET /scheduling/dood?scheduleVersionId= and downloads file. Sticky first column (character names) and sticky header row for large tables. Vitest test: render DOOD table with 5 characters and 8 shoot days; assert W/H/T designations in correct cells; assert CSV export button triggers download.

Title: Build budget panel with above and below the line tables inline edit and running totals
Description: Create apps/web/src/budget/components/BudgetPanel.tsx. Data from useBudgetQuery(scriptVersionId). Three sections: Above The Line, Below The Line, Post Production. Each section: table with columns Account Code, Description, Qty, Unit, Rate, Amount; inline edit on click (contentEditable cells with save-on-blur and Enter key). 'Add Line' button per section: adds blank row with account code picker (Select with all codes grouped by category). Running totals row at bottom of each section (bold). Grand total summary: ATL + BTL + POST + Contingency (10%) + Grand Total. Locked budget: all cells read-only; locked banner. Apollo optimistic updates for edits. Vitest test: render with 5 lines; edit one amount; assert updateBudgetLine mutation called; assert total updates correctly.

Title: Build publish button confirmation modal and real-time saga status tracker with WebSocket updates
Description: Create apps/web/src/publish/components/PublishButton.tsx and SagaStatusTracker.tsx. PublishButton: primary button 'Publish to Production' visible to PRODUCER/ADMIN roles on script detail page. On click: open PublishConfirmationModal showing current version hash, word count, scene count, and checkbox 'I confirm this script version is ready for production'. On confirm: call publishScript(scriptVersionId) GraphQL mutation which starts Temporal workflow; redirect to SagaStatusPage. SagaStatusPage: poll publishSagaStatus(sagaId) GraphQL query every 5s (or update via WebSocket notification). Status stepper: Submitting → Validating Policy → Extracting Breakdown → Computing Schedule → Computing Budget → Awaiting Approval → Published. Each step: pending (grey), in-progress (blue spinner), done (green tick), failed (red x with error message). On failure: show 'Compensation in progress…' step. Vitest test: render SagaStatusPage with mocked polling; assert stepper progresses through steps.
Parent: As a writer or producer I can publish a script to production which triggers the full seven-step Temporal approval saga

Title: Build approval inbox and detail view with script diff schedule delta and budget delta for approvers
Description: Create apps/web/src/approval/components/ApprovalInbox.tsx and ApprovalDetail.tsx. ApprovalInbox: list of pending approval_requests for current user (useApprovalInboxQuery). Each item: script title, step name (e.g. 'Legal Review'), deadline with countdown timer, saga status badge. Click → ApprovalDetail. ApprovalDetail: tabbed view with three tabs — (1) Script Changes: side-by-side diff of changed scenes (added lines green, removed lines red); (2) Schedule Delta: summary of added/removed shoot days + moved scenes; (3) Budget Delta: net ATL/BTL/POST change amount. Approve/Reject buttons: Approve calls approveSaga(requestId) mutation; Reject opens RejectModal requiring comment then calls rejectSaga(requestId, comment). After decision: redirect to inbox with success toast. Vitest test: render ApprovalDetail with mocked diff; click Approve; assert approveSaga mutation called.
Parent: As an approver I can receive review and act on approval requests with script version diff and schedule and budget deltas
